title: db.rs: migration jumps version without applying intermediate SQL
description:
LOCATION: src/db.rs:125-171, ensure_migrations()

FINDING: The migration loop is 'for v in version..CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION'. MIGRATIONS[0] is an empty string (version 0→1 is 'no migration needed'). The loop skips empty entries (line 160: '!MIGRATIONS[idx].is_empty()'). 

The bug: if a database was created at version 0 (before schema_meta existed) and then upgraded past version 1 to the current version, the loop correctly skips index 0. However, if a fresh database is created (schema_meta does not exist), the code at line 145-149 inserts schema_version = CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION (4) and returns Ok(()) immediately — skipping all migrations. This is intentional (the SCHEMA constant already includes all tables). 

The actual gap: MIGRATIONS[2] is 'ALTER TABLE relays ADD COLUMN persona TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT \'\''. If a DB was created at schema version 1 (before schema_meta existed, so 'version' is read as... wait — schema_meta itself is created by ensure_migrations, so a DB that predates schema_meta would have no schema_meta row and would hit the None arm at line 143, setting version to CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION and returning — never running migrations). That means an existing DB from before schema_meta was introduced will permanently skip all migrations, leaving the relays.persona column and personas.did_key/recovery_pubkey columns absent.

IMPACT: Operators who upgrade from a version before schema_meta was introduced will silently get a broken DB where relay subscriptions and DID features don't work, with no error at startup.
close_reason: Fixed: migration starts at version 0, applies all intermediate SQL (same fix as r9da.1)
